Home»Sport»Paris Olympics: When Team SA is in action on Monday

Here are all the times when Team SA athletes will be in action at the ever-entertaining Paris Olympics on Monday, 29 July.

The Paris Olympics has got off to an enthralling start, while Team SA has alreadybanked one medal, courtesy of the bronze secured by the Blitzboks

There could be more to add on Monday, with hopes high that South Africa could secure their first gold medal through swimming sensation Tatjana Smith.

Pieter Coetze is also a realistic medal contender in the pool later on Monday.

BADMINTON

Women’s Singles, Group H (2.50pm):Johanita Scoltz vs Jin Wei Goh (Malaysia)

CYCLING

Men’s MTB cross-country (2.10pm):Alan Hatherley

HOCKEY

Women’s Pool B (5.30pm):Team SA vs Argentina

RUGBY SEVENS

Women’s Pool B (2pm):Team SA vs Great Britain

SKATEBOARDING

Men’s street prelims (12noon):Brandon Valjalo

SURFING

Men’s round 3 (7pm):Jordy Smith vs Alonso Per Correa (Peru)

SWIMMING

Men’s 100m backstroke, final (9.19pm):Pieter Coetze

Women’s 100m breaststroke, final (9.25pm):Tatjana Smith

Tatjana Smith, formerly Schoenmaker,won her heat and semi-finalin the 100m at the Paris La Defense Arena on Sunday, touching the wall in both races on the day in 1min 05.00sec.

“I’m just so grateful swimming to be good times again,” the Team SA superstar said. “I haven’t been close to my time from Tokyo (1:04.82), so to be swimming my best times since then is an amazing feeling. I’m just happy to be enjoying every race and it doesn’t matter what times I do put up. It’s about the experience and how happy I am to be here doing what I’m doing.”

Coetze is still only 20, but joined the medals conversation for the men’s 100m backstroke. He was lightning quick in evening, breaking the African record with a 52.63 touch, for tie second and tied third overall heading into Monday night’s final.

Since 1992, South Africa has won 82 golds, 56 silvers and 54 bronzes.
